title: Prompt Injection, Tool Output Trust, Exfiltration, and Safe Rendering · Program Brief: Exfiltration Control
summary: Retrieved text claims to be system instructions, a tool result requests secrets, the agent follows embedded links, approval text is forged, and model Markdown reaches an unsafe renderer. Predict authority, model and API behavior, state, tool effects, budgets, privacy, safety, accessibility, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the exfiltration control increment in instruction-data separation and agent security gateway. Learners progress thr…
objectives: Constrain destination, data class, field flow, tool combinations, external communication, and covert channels with policy and audit evidence.
Treat tool names, content, links, files, citations, metadata, error text, and nested model output as untrusted typed data.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-ai-agent-python cumulative safe evaluated AI agent portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python interaction, tool, policy, state, memory,
evaluation, telemetry, and recovery models with original fixed in-memory fixtures only.
Provider APIs, Gemini models, native google-genai, google-adk, Pydantic, HTTP, credentials,
host files, code execution, browsers, databases, telemetry backends, load, cost, privacy review,
and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class AgentPolicy:
    stakeholder_task_id: str
    allowed_tool_names: tuple[str, ...]
    maximum_steps: int = 12
    maximum_tool_calls: int = 8
    approval_required_for_writes: bool = True


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class AgentEvidence:
    revision: str
    model_config_digest: str
    policy_digest: str
    evaluation_digest: str
    recovery_drill_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Safe evaluated Python AI agent portfolio ready"

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example agent engineering team handles case 6641 during a controlled support-operations rehearsal. Build deterministic Python evidence to separate instructions from data, resist direct and indirect injection, constrain tools, prevent exfiltration, and encode rendered output; last valid checkpoint, audit trail, kill switch, and rollback path must survive failure; then change model capability, API version, schema, or provider state and revalidate the adapter. Browser code uses pure bounded state machines and original fixed fixtures only. Real provider APIs, Gemini models, google-genai, google-adk, Pydantic native validation, HTTP, files, credentials, code execution, browsers, databases, telemetry backends, load, privacy review, and production effects require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Constrain destination, data class, field flow, tool combinations, external communication, and covert channels with policy and audit evidence. A defensible solution applies Exfiltration Control, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to instruction-data separation and agent security gateway.

content.code:
# Evidence: agentpy-worked-agentpy-injection-output-security-4
# Competency: agentpy-worked-agentpy-injection-output-security-4.
# Case 1655: a controlled support-operations rehearsal.
# Operating constraint: telemetry and memory must remain tenant-isolated, minimized, redacted, and attributable.
# Changed case: change one eval case, rubric, model version, token price, rate limit, or latency distribution and rerun release gates.
def worked_agentpy_injection_output_security_4(untrusted_inputs: int = 40, rejected_instructions: int = 18, admitted_data: int = 22, exfiltration_events: int = 0, unsafe_renderings: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked4 = "worked4-4dekrow-1655"
    assert evidence_variant_worked4
    evidence_axes_worked4 = (
        "authorityworked4",
        "interaction1655",
        "tool4dekrow",
        "stateworked41655",
        "evaluation1655worked4",
        "recovery4dekrow1655",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked4)) == 6
    if untrusted_inputs < 1 or rejected_instructions < 0 or admitted_data < 0:
        return False, untrusted_inputs, "security-input-count"
    if rejected_instructions + admitted_data != untrusted_inputs:
        return False, rejected_instructions, "instruction-data-reconciliation"
    if exfiltration_events > 0:
        return False, exfiltration_events, "data-exfiltration"
    if unsafe_renderings > 0:
        return False, unsafe_renderings, "unsafe-output-rendering"
    assert rejected_instructions + admitted_data == untrusted_inputs and exfiltration_events == 0
    return True, rejected_instructions, "secured-agent-boundary"

steps.title: Safe AI agents and Python Build Exfiltration Control evidence · Theory Exfiltration Control
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: agentpy-exfiltration-control-pifv1f" that uses case 3561 to separate instructions from data, resist direct and indirect injection, constrain tools, prevent exfiltration, and encode rendered output. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not contact provider APIs, invoke Gemini models, import or claim native google-genai, google-adk, or Pydantic behavior, open sockets, read or write host files, use credentials, execute generated code, launch browsers, reach databases or telemetry backends, or claim live privacy, load, safety, cost, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, google-genai 2.11.0, google-adk 2.4.0, Pydantic 2.13.4, fixed provider fixtures, fake transports, controlled local services, disposable cloud projects, representative eval sets, accessibility, privacy and security review, load, canary, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

content.code:
# Evidence: agentpy-exfiltration-control-pifv1f
# Competency: agentpy-exfiltration-control.
# Case 3561: a clean package, canary, and recovery drill.
# Operating constraint: sensitive actions require a trustworthy user approval bound to exact arguments.
# Changed case: change one eval case, rubric, model version, token price, rate limit, or latency distribution and rerun release gates.
def evidence_agentpy_exfiltration_control_pifv1f(untrusted_inputs: int = 40, rejected_instructions: int = 18, admitted_data: int = 22, exfiltration_events: int = 0, unsafe_renderings: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_pifv1f = "pifv1f-f1vfip-3561"
    assert evidence_variant_pifv1f
    evidence_axes_pifv1f = (
        "authoritypifv1f",
        "interaction3561",
        "toolf1vfip",
        "statepifv1f3561",
        "evaluation3561pifv1f",
        "recoveryf1vfip3561",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_pifv1f)) == 6
    if untrusted_inputs < 1 or rejected_instructions < 0 or admitted_data < 0:
        return False, untrusted_inputs, "security-input-count"
    if rejected_instructions + admitted_data != untrusted_inputs:
        return False, rejected_instructions, "instruction-data-reconciliation"
    if exfiltration_events > 0:
        return False, exfiltration_events, "data-exfiltration"
    if unsafe_renderings > 0:
        return False, unsafe_renderings, "unsafe-output-rendering"
    assert rejected_instructions + admitted_data == untrusted_inputs and exfiltration_events == 0
    return True, rejected_instructions, "secured-agent-boundary"
